problem_spec.summary: Classify all functions $f$ satisfying an additive_Cauchy / Pexider_type / Jensen_type identity on a specified domain (e.g., $Q_to_Q$, $R_to_R$, or interval_to_interval). Additional constraint_atoms (bounded/monotone/continuity_forced) force the wild solutions away and yield an affine (or linear) description.
givens: A domain model $D$ is chosen from {reals, rationals, integers, an interval} (parameter).
The function satisfies one of the archetype_families: additive_Cauchy, Pexider_type, or Jensen_type (parameterized).
At least one regularity/constraint_atom is assumed: bounded on an interval, monotone, or continuity_forced.
ask: Determine the full set of functions $f:D\to D$ satisfying the given functional equation and constraint_atoms.

parameter(s).name: domain_model
parameter(s).purpose: Chooses the ambient domain and affects which regularity implies continuity_forced.

parameter(s).name: regularity_guard
parameter(s).purpose: Selects which constraint_atom eliminates pathological solutions.

invariant(s).name: translation_invariance
invariant(s).statement: The equation implies invariance under translations or midpoints (e.g., $f(x+y)-f(x)-f(y)=0$ or Jensen midpoint condition).

invariant(s).name: rational_scaling
invariant(s).statement: From generate_recursion and equal_inputs one obtains $f(qx)=qf(x)$ for rational $q$ whenever the domain allows it.

invariant(s).name: interface_quantity_T
invariant(s).statement: Define the interface quantity $T=f(1)$ (or slope parameter); the equation forces $f(x)=Tx$ on $Q_to_Q$, and regularity_guard extends this to $R_to_R$ or interval_to_interval.

invariant(s).name: constraint_atoms_rigidity
invariant(s).statement: Constraint_atoms such as injective, surjective, bijective, bounded, or monotone are used to deduce continuity_forced (or to force fixed_points), eliminating wild additive_Cauchy solutions and enabling show_linearity.

essential_assumption(s): The domain_model is closed under required operations (addition and rational scaling as needed).
regularity_guard holds on a nontrivial interval/subset so that continuity_forced can be deduced when working over $R_to_R$.
uniqueness_certificate(s): The equation_form plus force_additivity determines $f$ on the rational skeleton by the single parameter $T=f(1)$ (so $f(q)=qT$ on $Q_to_Q$). Any regularity_guard from {bounded, monotone, injective, surjective, bijective} implies continuity_forced on a nontrivial interval, which uniquely extends the rational formula to $R_to_R$ / interval_to_interval. Thus the solution set is completely and uniquely classified by $T$.

technique(s).name: plug_special_values
technique(s).purpose: Obtain $f(0)$, $f(1)$, and other anchors that seed recursion.

technique(s).name: swap_variables
technique(s).purpose: Derive symmetry relations and cancellation identities that imply additivity.

technique(s).name: bounding_trap
technique(s).purpose: Use bounds to show the additive solution is continuous, hence linear.

technique(s).name: force_additivity
technique(s).purpose: Reduce the given equation_form to the additive_Cauchy skeleton by cancellations and standard substitutions.

technique(s).name: show_linearity
inputs: additive_Cauchy on $Q$ or on $R$ plus a regularity_guard
technique(s).purpose: Use bounded/monotone/continuity_forced to rule out pathological additive solutions and conclude $f(x)=Tx$ (or affine) on the full domain_model.

theorem(s).name: additive_Cauchy
theorem(s).role: Characterize solutions on $Q$ and under regularity on $R$.

theorem(s).name: Jensen_type
theorem(s).role: Relate midpoint convexity to linearity under boundedness.

theorem(s).name: Pexider_type
theorem(s).role: Reduce a three-function variant to the additive_Cauchy skeleton.

critical_trick(s): Use plug_special_values and equal_inputs to isolate $T=f(1)$ and force additivity.
Show that regularity_guard implies continuity_forced, hence show_linearity on $R_to_R$.
If the domain is mixed_domain, classify_piecewise and then stitch via the equation.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Check derived linear forms and verify functional identities symbolically.

library(s).name: random
library(s).reason: Random testing of candidate closed forms on sampled domain points.

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Symbolically derive additivity from the chosen equation_form (Pexider_type/Jensen_type) via plug_special_values, swap_variables, and equal_inputs; then prove the rational scaling formula $f(qx)=qf(x)$ by generate_recursion and check that the inferred linear form satisfies the original identity.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: For representative parameter choices and constraint_atoms (bounded/monotone/injective/surjective/bijective), run randomized tests on rational inputs and on sampled real inputs: verify the equation numerically for the candidate linear/affine forms and search for counterexamples on small finite grids.
